en: Learn English with fun facts, perfect for beginners.
Penguins live in very cold places like Antarctica.
The wind there is very strong and very cold.
To stay warm, penguins stand very close together.
This big group is called a huddle.
Penguins on the outside feel the cold first.
Slowly they move toward the middle.
Penguins in the middle become warmer and rest.
Then they move out again to help others.
This way everyone gets a warm place.
It is teamwork that helps penguins survive winter.
Would you huddle with others to stay warm?
